title: crepis crocea
intro: Crepis crocea, also known as the Safflower or Yellow Hawk's Beard, is a flowering plant species in the dandelion family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Crepis crocea include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and soothing skin irritations. The herbal preparations of Crepis crocea include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are often used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments.
